attacking_style: Favors late-race tactical attacks (1.5-3km to go) rather than long-range moves. Willing to attack on technical descents (28.6km from finish) and opportunistically contest intermediate bonuses far from finish (75km out), showing versatility beyond typical climber profile.
team_tactics: When protected, teams set tempo on climbs but Carapaz often forced to make own selections with 2-3km attacks. Post-INEOS transition to EF shows more breakaway hunting for stage wins rather than sustained GC support. Team sometimes paces conservatively when defending jersey position.
terrain_preference: Technical descents where he attacks aggressively (2019-style descent attacks at 28km out). Medium-length climbs allowing late attacks (1.5-3km). Exposed on ultra-steep pitches (Vall Ter 2000, Picon Blanco) and wet/technical descents under pressure (crashed Catalunya, dropped Tour descent).
vulnerability: Wet-weather handling catastrophic (crashed Catalunya descent, lost 2:56 Vuelta). Drops on technical descents under race pressure despite attacking on them when fresh. Time trialing limits podium ceilings. Collapses inside final 3km when overtrained/ill (Giro finale). Struggles immediately post-Olympics.
key_patterns: Peaks late in Grand Tours: stage win Vuelta stage 15, attempted comeback after struggles (2 instances)
Post-Olympic fitness significantly compromised: struggled Vuelta 2021, dropped Lombardia 2023 (2 instances)
Attacks on descents when fresh but drops on descents under sustained pressure (4 instances)
Hunts intermediate bonuses and breakaway KOM points unusually aggressively for GC climber (3 instances)
Final 1.5-3km attack range when contesting stages, tactical patience before committing (3 instances)
Wet conditions catastrophic weakness: crashes and massive time losses (2+ instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Catalunya showed KOM-hunting breakaway aggression but Lombardia exposed gap between championship fitness and monument level. Mid-tier domestique role emerging post-EF move.
